proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with egfr_mutation=1, those receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving osimertinib.
Among patients with kras_g12c=1, those receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving sotorasib.
Among patients with brca2_mutation=1, those receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving olaparib.
Among patients with tmb_high=1, those receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving pembrolizumab.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square: response on/off osimertinib within egfr_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: Among EGFR-mutant patients (n=6505), response rate on osimertinib was 0.164 vs 0.166 off (chi-square p=0.87). Direction is essentially null, not the expected positive effect.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square: response on/off sotorasib within kras_g12c==1
analyses.result_summary: Among KRAS G12C-mutant patients (n=6346), response on sotorasib was 0.175 vs 0.166 off (p=0.43). Directionally positive but not significant; effect much smaller than expected for a matched targeted therapy.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square: response on/off olaparib within brca2_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: Among BRCA2-mutant patients (n=1500), response on olaparib was 0.146 vs 0.168 off (p=0.35). Direction is opposite of expected and not significant.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square: response on/off pembrolizumab within tmb_high==1
analyses.result_summary: Among TMB-high patients (n=14238), response on pembrolizumab was 0.197 vs 0.167 off (chi-square p=5.4e-6). Strong positive effect of pembrolizumab in TMB-high subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps amplifies the effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (positive pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction in logistic regression).
Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response rate; among those with pdl1_tps < 0.5 it does not.
objective_response rate differs across smoking_status categories (never vs former vs current).
The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is greater in current/former smokers than in never-smokers.
objective_response rate differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction coefficient = +0.591 (z=5.24, p=1.6e-7). Strong positive interaction: pembrolizumab benefit grows with PD-L1 TPS.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square within pdl1_tps>=0.5 and pdl1_tps<0.5
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 high (n=10749): response on pembro 0.209 vs 0.156 off (p=7.7e-13). PD-L1 low (n=39251): 0.164 vs 0.166 (p=0.59). Pembrolizumab efficacy is essentially confined to the PD-L1 high stratum.
Response rates: current 0.172, former 0.169, never 0.164 (chi-square p=0.27). No significant overall main effect of smoking status on response.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square: pembro effect within each smoking_status
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab effect (on - off): former smokers +0.011 (p=0.017), current +0.012 (p=0.048), never +0.001 (p=0.91). Benefit is concentrated in former/current smokers; absent in never-smokers.
Adenocarcinoma 0.167 vs squamous 0.174 (chi-square p=0.08). Borderline, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stk11_mutation reduces (or abolishes) the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (negative pembrolizumab x stk11 interaction).
keap1_mutation reduces the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (negative pembrolizumab x keap1 interaction).
tp53_mutation modifies the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response.
In PD-L1 high (>=0.5) patients without stk11_mutation, pembrolizumab strongly increases response; in PD-L1 high patients with stk11_mutation the benefit is attenuated/absent.

analyses.result_summary: pembro x stk11 interaction = -0.211 (z=-3.13, p=0.0018). In STK11 wildtype: pembro 0.177 vs 0.162 off (p=9.7e-5); in STK11 mutant: 0.158 vs 0.173 (p=0.08, slight numerical reversal). Negative interaction confirmed.

pembro x keap1 interaction = -0.058 (p=0.36), not significant. Subgroup: KEAP1 wt pembro benefit 0.011 (p=0.0024), KEAP1 mut +0.003 (p=0.73). Numerically attenuated but interaction term not significant.
pembro x tp53 interaction = +0.028 (p=0.56), not significant. No evidence TP53 mutation modifies pembrolizumab efficacy here.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square within pdl1_tps>=0.5 by stk11_mutation status
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 high & STK11 wt (n=9149): pembro 0.217 vs 0.154 (p=5.6e-15). PD-L1 high & STK11 mut (n=1600): 0.163 vs 0.168 (p=0.86). STK11 mutation abolishes the PD-L1-driven pembrolizumab benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate (positive logistic coefficient).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate (negative logistic coefficient).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response rate.

Across remaining routine labs (hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, electrolytes, blood counts), no lab will show a strong main-effect association with objective_response after the inflammatory/nutritional labs are accounted for.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ albumin_g_dl); also t-test responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin 3.821 in responders vs 3.798 in non-responders (t-test p=1.3e-4). Univariate logistic coef = +0.091 per g/dL (p=1.4e-4). Higher albumin associated with higher response.
Mean CRP 5.78 in responders vs 6.15 in non-responders (t-test p=4.2e-4). Logistic coef = -0.0049 per mg/L (p=6.9e-4). Higher CRP associated with lower response.
Mean LDH 224.3 vs 223.6 (responders vs non-responders), t-test p=0.50; logistic coef +0.0001 (p=0.50). No significant LDH effect, contrary to expectations from many trials.
Mean NLR 3.48 in responders vs 3.50 in non-responders, p=0.38; logistic coef -0.005 (p=0.38). Not significant.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic regression of response on each remaining lab
analyses.result_summary: Hemoglobin, AlkPhos, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, sodium, calcium, platelets, WBC, ANC, ALC: all p>0.05 univariately (smallest p=0.08 for AST). Confirms hypothesis: routine labs other than albumin/CRP have no detectable main effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative logistic coefficient).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are each associated with lower objective_response.
The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is smaller in patients with higher ecog_ps (negative ecog x pembrolizumab interaction).

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ ecog_ps); response by ECOG group
analyses.result_summary: Response by ECOG: 0=21.1%, 1=15.7%, 2=11.1%. Logistic coef = -0.375 per ECOG point (p=1.7e-93). Strongest single predictor of response.
Mean WL 3.38% in responders vs 3.93% in non-responders (p=2.8e-35). Logistic coef = -0.039 per % weight loss (p=1.8e-32). Strong negative association.

fatigue_grade coef=-0.022 (p=0.04, weakly significant). pain_nrs p=0.78, dyspnea_grade p=0.49, cough_grade p=0.26, appetite_loss_grade p=0.35: all null. Only fatigue trends downward; other patient-reported symptoms have no main effect.
pembro x ecog interaction = -0.036 (p=0.32). Not significant. Pembrolizumab benefit does not appear to scale with ECOG within this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv=1 is associated with lower objective_response than non-stage IV.
has_brain_mets=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
Liver, bone, adrenal, contralateral lung mets, and pleural/pericardial effusions each individually depress objective_response rate.
The number of metastatic sites (sum of brain/liver/bone/adrenal/contralateral) is inversely related to objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV: 15.4% vs non-stage IV: 19.6% (chi-square p=1.1e-33). Strong negative association.
Brain mets: 14.4% vs no brain mets: 17.7% (p=2.0e-18). Strong negative association.

analyses.code: chi-square: each metastatic site x response
analyses.result_summary: Liver mets p=0.34 (+0.005), bone mets p=0.18 (+0.006), adrenal mets p=0.39 (-0.005), pleural eff p=0.89 (+0.001), pericardial eff p=0.61 (+0.005), contralateral lung mets p=0.98. None significant. Only brain mets and stage IV carry prognostic signal among the anatomic features.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ count of [brain, liver, bone, adrenal, contralateral])
analyses.result_summary: Response by mets-count: 0=17.7%, 1=16.4%, 2=16.5%, 3=14.9%, 4=14.2%. Coef=-0.057 (p=1.0e-4). Significant decline with greater metastatic burden.

proposed_hypotheses.text: objective_response rate differs across race_ethnicity categories.
objective_response rate differs across insurance_type categories (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).
rural_residence=1 patients have lower objective_response than urban patients.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have higher objective_response rate than males.
Across age_years, education_years, smoking_pack_years, and bmi, none has a meaningful main-effect association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Asian 17.7%, Black 17.8%, Hispanic 17.2%, Other 18.5%, White 16.5% (chi-square p=0.045). Borderline; the lone signal is a small reduction in white patients vs others.
Medicaid 16.1%, Medicare 16.8%, Private 17.2%, Uninsured 17.0% (p=0.23). Not significant.
Rural 16.7% vs non-rural 17.0% (p=0.50). No effect.
Female 17.5% vs male 16.4% (chi-square p=5.9e-4). Females have a small but reliable response advantage.
age p=0.67, education p=0.40, pack-years p=0.76, BMI p=0.35. All null. None of these continuous demographic/lifestyle features carries a main-effect signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression, the pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps and pembrolizumab x tmb_high interactions remain positive, the pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction remains negative, while the targeted therapy x matched biomarker interactions (osimertinib x egfr, sotorasib x kras_g12c, olaparib x brca2) remain null after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss, albumin, and CRP each retain independent prognostic signal in a multivariable model.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ pembro + pdl1 + pembro:pdl1 + tmb + pembro:tmb + stk11 + pembro:stk11 + ecog + weight_loss + albumin + crp + stage4 + brain_mets + sex_female + osi*egfr + soto*kras + olap*brca)
analyses.result_summary: pembro x pdl1 = +0.596 (p=1.6e-7); pembro x tmb_high = +0.174 (p=9.4e-4); pembro x stk11 = -0.204 (p=0.003). osi x egfr = -0.021 (p=0.74), soto x kras = +0.032 (p=0.57), olap x brca = -0.187 (p=0.18). All targeted-therapy x biomarker interactions remain null. Pseudo R^2 = 0.021 overall.

analyses.code: Same multivariable Logit; inspect prognostic coefficients
analyses.result_summary: ECOG -0.382 (p=1e-95), stage_iv -0.302 (p=1.6e-34), brain_mets -0.257 (p=8.8e-19), weight_loss -0.039 (p=1.7e-33), albumin +0.092 (p=1.2e-4), CRP -0.005 (p=4.2e-4), sex_female +0.081 (p=8e-4). All remain independently significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 23 SNPs (snp_rs*), no SNP has a Bonferroni-significant association with objective_response (Bonferroni alpha = 0.05/23 = 0.00217).
Prior treatments (chemotherapy, radiation, surgery, immunotherapy, targeted therapy) and number of prior_lines_of_therapy do not have main-effect associations with objective_response on this line.
Common comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, heart failure, autoimmune disease, ILD history, prior malignancy, depression/anxiety) do not affect objective_response rate.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ snp) for each of 23 SNPs; rank by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p-values: snp_rs7412 p=0.018 (coef +0.10), snp_rs1050828 p=0.031 (coef -0.099). 2/23 SNPs nominally <0.05 (consistent with chance), 0/23 below Bonferroni 0.00217 threshold. No SNP is reliably associated with response.

analyses.code: chi-square per prior-treatment indicator; logit on prior_lines_of_therapy
analyses.result_summary: Prior chemo p=0.07, prior radiation p=0.90, prior surgery p=0.36, prior immunotherapy p=0.39, prior targeted p=0.30. prior_lines_of_therapy logit coef -0.012 (p=0.24). All null.
Diabetes p=0.76, HTN p=0.72, COPD p=0.38, CKD p=0.22, heart failure p=0.89, autoimmune p=0.42, ILD p=0.27, prior malignancy p=0.45, depression/anxiety p=0.67. All null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is greater in females (sex_female=1) than in males (positive pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction).
Within PD-L1 high (pdl1_tps>=0.5) patients, pembrolizumab raises response in current and former smokers but not in never-smokers (smoking modifies the pembrolizumab benefit even within high-PD-L1).
There is a three-way pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps x stk11_mutation interaction such that the PD-L1-driven pembrolizumab benefit is greatest in STK11 wildtype and absent in STK11 mutant.

A refined multivariable model containing pembrolizumab and its PD-L1, TMB, and STK11 interactions plus prognostic covariates (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, weight_loss, albumin, CRP, sex_female) preserves all interaction signs and significances and provides the most coherent description of objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: pembro x sex_female interaction = +0.146 (z=3.04, p=0.0024). Stratified: in males pembro effect 0.000 (p=0.94); in females +0.021 (p=2.6e-5). Pembrolizumab benefit appears in females and is absent in males.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square within pdl1_tps>=0.5 by smoking_status
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 high & former smokers: pembro 0.205 vs 0.170 (p=5e-4). Current smokers: 0.241 vs 0.140 (p=7.8e-13). Never-smokers: 0.159 vs 0.132 (p=0.14). Even within PD-L1 high tumors, pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in current/former smokers.

Three-way pembro x pdl1 x stk11 interaction coef = -0.388 (p=0.22), not significant. The two-way pembro x pdl1 interaction (+0.644, p<0.001) and pembro x stk11 trend (-0.088, p=0.46 in this expanded model) are present but the three-way effect is not statistically supported, though direction is consistent with PD-L1-high + STK11-wt receiving greatest pembrolizumab benefit observed in stratified analysis.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ pembro + pdl1 + pembro:pdl1 + tmb_high + pembro:tmb + stk11 + pembro:stk11 + ecog + weight_loss + albumin + crp + stage4 + brain_mets + sex_female)
analyses.result_summary: Final refined model: pembro x pdl1 = +0.595 (p=1.6e-7); pembro x tmb = +0.174 (p=9.7e-4); pembro x stk11 = -0.203 (p=0.003); ECOG -0.382 (p=1e-95); stage_iv -0.302 (p=1.6e-34); brain_mets -0.257 (p=9e-19); weight_loss -0.039 (p=1.8e-32); albumin +0.092 (p=1.2e-4); CRP -0.005 (p=4.6e-4); sex_female +0.081 (p=8.4e-4); pembrolizumab main effect alone -0.131 (p=0.005). Pseudo R^2 = 0.021. All target interactions retain sign and significance.
